author: @jonnyswalk_7
text: Really enjoyed the video Rob as always. Fantastic little project 👍 - Warmest wishes from Wales 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁷󠁬󠁳󠁿 - Jonathan 😊 👍

author: @BoB4jjjjs
text: I can't play anything else, but I can play drums much to my surprise, but I am not going to wreck my speakers to make a tongue drum.  However, I am keeping my eyes open for some good scrapped wood.  Good wood for drums is not often thrown out though :-((

author: @czechyorker
text: Frequency? Hmm - I smell Tesla and his famous quote. Like the finished product.

author: @olymk2
text: After making these and doing the ink video's, you should do a few videos on making stain's / paints to finish the instruments.

author: @NorthernKitty
text: A speaker case hadn't occurred to me - that's a great idea for a ready-made solution!  Now I'll be keeping my eye open for other forms of wood boxes in thrift shops. 😊  We also have a flooring manufacturer near where I live... I should see if they still give away their scraps.  People used to go there to get hardwood for their fireplace for a hotter, longer burning fire.  That would be a great source for the tongues for this.  This is really motivating me to finally clear out my shed and turn it into a woodshop.  😋

author: @pulesjet
text: Great information but I have no interest in music.

author: @rastamanralph6670
text: You could put a bit of rubber tube round the bolt in a bigger hole and leave the bolts slack a bit to make more vibration.

author: @jamest.5001
text:
I have tried soo hard to stop picking up things that the neighbors are tossing out, stuff I GOTTA HAVE, can't live without! But ends up sitting in a pile for a few years!    
Anyway getting to the point here, How much power can you get from this thing! Ha-ha!

replies.text: Less than you put in lol.
